# Manage access with roles

@include 'rbac/role-definition.mdx'

Once you create a role, you add principals and grants to it.
The principals inherit the grants.
You can optionally add grant scopes to the role to configure inheritance across multiple scopes.

## Create a role

Roles let you map grant strings or permissions to principals, which are users, groups, and managed groups.

Complete the following steps to create a role:

<Tabs>
<Tab heading="UI" group="ui">

1. Log in to Boundary.
1. Navigate to an org, and then select **Roles** on the navigation pane.
1. Click **New** or **New Role**.
1. Complete the following fields:
   - **Name**: (Optional) Enter an optional name for the role for identification purposes.
   - **Description**: (Optional) Enter an optional description of the role for identification purposes.
1. Click **Save**.

  Boundary creates the role and takes you to the role's **Settings** page.

To view a cheat sheet to help you create roles, refer to [Example roles](/boundary/docs/rbac/example-roles).

</Tab>
<Tab heading="CLI" group="cli">

1. Log in to Boundary.
1. Use the following command to create a role:
  ```shell-session
  $ boundary roles create -scope-id o_1234567890 \
      -description 'An example role' \
      -name 'Example role'
  ```
  You can use any of the following attributes when you create a role:

  @include 'attributes/roles/create.mdx'

To view a cheat sheet to help you create roles, refer to [Example roles](/boundary/docs/rbac/example-roles).

</Tab>
<Tab heading="Terraform" group="terraform">

Before you create a role, ensure that the principals you want to assign to the role exist.
Refer to [Terraform patterns for users and auth methods](/boundary/docs/deploy/terraform-patterns/terraform-users-and-auth-methods) for more information.

You create the role using the `boundary_role` resource.

The following example adds the "listonly" role to the `boundary_scope` org scope.

```hcl
resource "boundary_role" "listonly" {
  name          = "listonly"
  description   = "A list only role"
  scope_id      = boundary_scope.org.id
}
```

To view a cheat sheet to help you create roles, refer to [Example roles](/boundary/docs/rbac/example-roles).

</Tab>
</Tabs>

## Assign principals to a role

You can grant users, groups, and managed groups permissions to perform actions by assigning them to a role.

Complete the following steps to assign principals to a role:

<Tabs>
<Tab heading="UI" group="ui">

1. Log in to Boundary.
1. Select **Roles** in the navigation pane, and then select the role you want to assign principals to.
1. Click the **Principals** tab.
1. Click the **Add Principals** button, or select it from the **Manage** dropdown.
1. Select one or more users, groups, or managed groups to associate with the role.
1. Click **Add Principals**.

  Boundary adds the principals to the role.
  The role's **Principals** page should now show the principals you selected.

</Tab>

<Tab heading="CLI" group="cli">

1. Log in to Boundary.
1. Use the following command to assign principals to a role:
  ```shell-session
  $ boundary roles add-principals -id r_22sVJoKZj3 \
      -principal g_qu2V5QMGQD
  ```
  You can use any of the following attributes when you assign principals a role:

  @include 'attributes/roles/add-principals.mdx'

</Tab>

<Tab heading="Terraform" group="terraform">

You can assign principals with the `principal_ids` attribute.

The following example adds the `boundary_user.foo` and `boundary_group.bar` principals to the role.

```hcl
resource "boundary_role" "listonly" {
  name          = "listonly"
  description   = "A list only role"
  principal_ids = [boundary_user.foo.id,boundary_group.bar.id]
  scope_id      = boundary_scope.org.id
}
```

</Tab>
</Tabs>

## Assign grants to a role

Grants describe the actions that the principals are allowed to perform.
You can add grants to a role either by manually defining grant strings or by using the UI to select from pre-defined templates.

Complete the following steps to assign grants to a role:

<Tabs>
<Tab heading="UI" group="ui">

1. Log in to Boundary.
1. In the navigation pane, select **Roles**, and then select the role you want to assign grants to.
1. On the role's **Settings** page, click the **Grants** tab.
1. Choose how you want to add grants:
   - To manually add or modify grants:
     1. Click **Edit Grants**.
     1. Enter or modify grant strings in the editor. Enter each grant string on a different line.
   - To add pre-defined grant templates:
     1. Click **Add Grant Templates**.
     1. In the template list, select the grant strings to add.
     1. Click **Add Grant Templates**.
1. Click **Save** to apply your changes.

To view common use cases for grants based on job function, refer to [Common use cases](/boundary/docs/rbac/use-cases).

</Tab>

<Tab heading="CLI" group="cli">

1. Log in to Boundary.
1. Use the following command to assign grants to a role:
  ```shell-session
  $ boundary roles add-grants -id r_22sVJoKZj3 \
      -grant 'ids=*;type=*;actions=no-op,list'
  ```
  You can use any of the following attributes when you assign grants a role:

  @include 'attributes/roles/add-grants.mdx'

To view common use cases for grants based on job function, refer to [Common use cases](/boundary/docs/rbac/use-cases).

</Tab>

<Tab heading="Terraform" group="terraform">

You assign grants using the `grant_strings` attribute.

```hcl
resource "boundary_role" "listonly" {
  name          = "listonly"
  description   = "A list only role"
  principal_ids = [boundary_user.foo.id,boundary_group.bar.id]
  grant_strings = ["ids=*;type=*;actions=no-op,list"]
  scope_id      = boundary_scope.org.id
}
```

To view common use cases for grants based on job function, refer to [Common use cases](/boundary/docs/rbac/use-cases).

</Tab>
</Tabs>

## Add grant scopes

You can assign roles to multiple scopes to configure role inheritance.
Roles can have a combination of the following grant scopes:

- `this` (this scope)
- `children` (all direct children of the assigned scope, `global` and org scopes only)
- `descendants` (all descendants of a scope, `global only`)
- ID (such as `o_v2MpV4vBHN` or `p_0vfvaQPwhD`. Roles accept multiple grant scope IDs)

Boundary automatically assigns grants the `this` scope, when you create them.

Complete the following steps to add grant scopes to a role:

<Tabs>
<Tab heading="UI" group="ui">

1. Log in to Boundary.
1. Select **Roles** in the navigation pane, and then select the role you want to add grant scopes to.
1. Click the **Scopes** tab.
1. From the **Manage** dropdown, select **Manage Scopes**.
1. Select from the following:
   - **Add this scope**: Adds the current scope to the grant.
   - **Add all children**: Adds all direct children of the current scope and can only be used with global and org scopes.
   - **Add all descendants**: Adds all descendants of the current scope and can only be used with the global scope.
1. To add additional scopes:
   1. Click **Manage custom scopes**.
   1. Select any additional scopes that you want to assign to the grant.
   1. Click **Save**.
1. Click **Save**.

  Boundary assigns the grant scopes to the role.

</Tab>

<Tab heading="CLI" group="cli">

1. Log in to Boundary.
1. Use the following command to add grant scopes:
  ```shell-session
  $ boundary roles add-grant-scopes -id r_22sVJoKZj3 \
      -grant-scope-id "children"
  ```
  You can use any of the following attributes when you add grant scopes:

  @include 'attributes/roles/add-grant-scopes.mdx'

</Tab>

<Tab heading="Terraform" group="terraform">

You can declare scope inheritance with the `grant_scope_ids` attribute.

The following example adds the assigned scope's ID with `this`, the ID of a project scope, and the direct children of the role's scope to the role. This includes projects within `org_one` in this example.

```hcl
resource "boundary_role" "listonly" {
  name            = "listonly"
  description     = "A list only role"
  principal_ids   = [boundary_user.foo.id,boundary_group.bar.id]
  grant_strings   = ["ids=*;type=*;actions=no-op,list"]
  scope_id        = boundary_scope.org.id
  grant_scope_ids = ["this","children"]
}
```

</Tab>
</Tabs>

## Next steps

To manage access to resources dynamically, refer to [Manage principals](/boundary/docs/rbac/users/manage-users-groups) and [Filter managed groups](/boundary/docs/rbac/users/managed-groups).

## More information

- To better understand Boundary's permissions model, refer to [Permissions in Boundary](/boundary/docs/rbac).
- For more specific information about the roles resource, refer to the [Roles](/boundary/docs/domain-model/roles) domain model topic.
- To learn more about creating or managing roles, refer to the CLI [`roles`](/boundary/docs/commands/roles) topic or the API [Role service](/boundary/api-docs/role-service) topic.
- To view a cheat sheet to help you create roles, refer to [Example roles](/boundary/docs/rbac/example-roles).
- To view common use cases for grants based on job function, refer to [Common use cases](/boundary/docs/rbac/use-cases).
